title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
How to Increase Income Source?
Relying on just one paycheck in this fast-paced world can be shaky, especially when life throws surprises like recessions or slow markets. That’s why learning how to increase income sources has become essential for long-term stability and wealth-building. When you diversify what you earn, you’re not only reducing risk but also giving yourself more room to save, invest, and grow financially. Let’s explore different ways to increase your income and how each one can benefit you.
Why is Increasing Your Income Important?
Today, people seek greater stability and rapid financial growth to fulfil their diverse needs. Here’s why increasing income sources is important:
Rising Inflation
Over the years, the prices of fuels, cooking gas, and everyday goods have risen significantly faster than salaries, disrupting household budgets and diminishing savings. With the rising cost of living and stagnant income, maintaining the quality of life and saving for the future seems difficult. To bridge the gap between earnings and expenses, diversifying income sources is important.
Raising Healthcare and Education Costs
Healthcare expenses in India are expected to increase to 13% in 2025 due to high hospitalisation charges and costly medical treatments. The cost of education has risen significantly (11-12% each year) in private schools and colleges. A single income source may not be enough to manage these necessary expenses.
Requirement During Emergency
Unexpected situations such as job loss, medical issues, or sudden hospital expenses can hit hard, as many people have seen in recent years. Having a passive income source acts as a safety net, giving you financial breathing room when life takes an unexpected turn.
Enhances Your Financial Coherence
Multiple income streams help you move toward your financial goals much faster. Whether you're planning a vacation, buying a home, or building a retirement corpus, extra earnings make these targets easier to achieve. It also helps you grow your wealth through varied investment plans, strengthening your overall financial stability.
Financial Freedom
Financial freedom comes easier when you have more than one way to earn. Extra income helps you save for both small treats and big milestones without constant financial stress. When you invest these earnings prudently, they grow over time and push you closer to your goals. Multiple income streams also give you confidence and room to explore new opportunities, from learning skills to planning early retirement.
Smart Ways to Increase Your Income
With digitisation, there are now multiple ways to increase income sources to support the daily standard of living. Suggested below are some ways to generate passive income:
Real Estate Investments
If you already have some savings, real estate may be a dependable way to grow your money over time. Buying property to rent out can give you regular passive income, and the value of the property itself may rise with time.
Commercial real estate often brings steadier returns than residential property. It tends to be less volatile, especially when pooled with others through fractional ownership, where you co-own part of a property and share in rental income with fellow investors.
Another accessible option is REITs (Real Estate Investment Trusts), which let you invest in large commercial properties using relatively small amounts, without buying them outright. REITs trade on stock exchanges like shares and distribute most of their rental earnings to investors as dividends while also offering potential for price appreciation.
Fixed Deposits and Bonds
Fixed Deposits remain a popular choice for low-risk investors because they’re simple, stable, and offer assured returns. Many banks provide competitive rates, and you also get perks like flexible tenure options, periodic interest payouts with non-cumulative deposits, and tax benefits on 5-year tax-saving FDs under Section 80C.
Bonds are equally easy to understand. You’re essentially lending money to a company or the government for a set time. In exchange, you receive regular interest and your principal back at maturity. They offer predictable returns, and investment-grade bonds often provide attractive returns, making them a solid option for steady, low-volatility income.
Invest in Unit Linked Insurance Plans (ULIPs)
ULIPs blend life insurance and market-linked returns into a single product. A portion of your premium provides insurance coverage, while the rest is invested in market-linked funds such as equity or debt. The maturity benefit received from a ULIP depends on how the market performs over time.
You may invest in ULIPs, as they not only ensure financial protection but also help in growing your savings with market-linked returns.
Start a Side Hustle
Starting a side hustle may be one of the easiest ways to add a new income stream without major upfront costs. With just your skills, interest, some time, and an internet connection, you can tap into flexible earning options like freelance writing, bookkeeping, social media management, virtual assistance, or translation work. These gigs not only boost your income but also help you build valuable experience. The key is to set healthy boundaries and maintain a steady routine to prevent burnout. When you treat your main job as financial support and not your entire identity, you can balance both roles while working toward steady growth.
Start an Online Business
Starting an online business is one of the most accessible ways to boost your income today. There are multiple options available:
Many people turn to affiliate marketing, where you promote a company’s products on your blogs or posts on social media and earn a commission on each sale.
Social media influencing is on the rise, with micro-influencers able to attract brands looking for collaborations on various digital platforms. The key is choosing a niche you genuinely enjoy, whether it’s food, beauty, finance, or career tips, and creating content that connects with your audience.
You may also build digital products like e-books, online courses, or templates. Once created, these assets can generate regular passive income long after the initial hard work is done.
Freelance Your Skills
Freelancing is a practical way to increase your income if you already have strong skills: whether it’s writing, designing, coding, or managing websites. Clients generally want proof of your work, so having a simple portfolio or website that showcases your best projects can make a big difference. By offering your expertise on a project basis, you can choose the work you enjoy and often earn more than a traditional job would pay. It’s a flexible path that lets you turn your skills into a steady, independent income.
Offer Online Courses or Coaching
Thanks to digitisation, teaching online is another way to increase income sources. There’s always someone looking to learn, be it maths, English, cooking, design, or even soft skills. You don’t need much to begin, just a phone or laptop and a couple of hours a day. Over time, you can even expand by creating digital products such as e-books, courses, or templates that generate passive income. With consistency, this simple side gig can grow into a full-fledged online business.
Benefits of Increasing Your Income
Here are some benefits of having more than one source of income:
Reducing Stress
When you have more than one earning option, you’ll be in a better position to handle daily living costs, health issues, and fulfil financial goals. The better your finances are, the easier your fight will be in any sudden emergency, such as losing your primary source of income or incurring substantial hospital bills.
Making Your Income More Stable
Having more than one income stream makes your earnings steadier. When your money comes from different places, such as a small business, freelance work, or investment plans, you’re not dependent on just one source. This variety also helps you manage your finances more efficiently, pick up new skills, and build stronger professional connections. Overall, it adds stability while giving you more control over how you work and grow.
Assisting You in Reaching Your Goals
Multiple income streams make it easier to move closer to your financial goals, from short-term ones, such as a vacation, to long-term milestones like building a retirement corpus or buying a home. Extra earnings allow you to put more money into saving plans and explore better investment options, helping your fund grow faster.
How to Generate Income with Low Investment?
You can also create wealth with low investments. Here are some ways to achieve that:
Capital Markets
Capital markets provide opportunities to earn money with small investments. Here, you can buy shares of companies or invest in debt instruments like bonds that pay regular interest. While market movements do carry some risk, thoughtful planning and informed choices can help you grow your money over time.
Mutual Funds
Mutual funds pool money from many investors, and a professional fund manager invests it across stocks, bonds, or other assets. You contribute your amount, and the fund handles the rest. The best part is that you can start affordably through an SIP, adjust your investments as goals change, and even save taxes with ELSS under Section 80C. It’s an easy and flexible way to increase your savings.
Company Fixed Deposits
Company FDs are fixed-term deposits offered by NBFCs and certain financial institutions, where you park your money for a set period at a pre-decided interest rate. They offer attractive returns, with tenures ranging from a few months to several years. It’s wise to check the company has strong fundamentals and a reliable credit rating before you invest, as safety depends on the issuer’s stability.
National Pension Scheme
The National Pension System (NPS) is a retirement-focused investment option that helps you build a long-term corpus. Your contributions are spread across equities, government securities, and corporate bonds, allowing you to choose the allocation based on your comfort with risk. NPS also offers tax benefits and comes with market-linked growth, making it a practical and low-cost way to save for the future.
Government Schemes
Government schemes include various investment avenues backed by the government. These investment schemes can offer guaranteed returns and also tax-benefits in certain cases. Some of the popular types of government schemes include Public Provident Fund, National Savings Certificates, Kisan Vikas Patra, etc.
Pension Plans
A pension plan is a retirement-oriented long-term savings scheme, where you set aside money regularly to build up a retirement fund. Once you retire, you can use the accumulated retirement fund to generate annuity income. You can choose from various options such as life insurance annuity plans, NPS, Atal Pension Yojana, Senior Citizen Saving Scheme, etc. Early contributions benefit from compounding, helping you accumulate a good corpus with time. Many plans also qualify for tax deductions under Sections 80C, 80CCC, and 80CCD, which makes retirement planning tax-efficient, too.
Key Takeaways
You need to increase income sources to beat inflation, handle hospitalisation and education costs, save money for emergencies, and fulfil all financial goals without stress.
Investing in real estate, FDs, bonds, or ULIPs is one of the answers to ‘how to increase an income source’.
You may use the internet to start a side hustle, teach online, start an online business, or freelance your skills to earn passive income.
When you have more than one income source, you’re able to save more, achieve your financial goals easily, and lead a stress-free life.
Some low investment plans to create a corpus are investing in the capital market, mutual funds, or company fixed deposits.
Government schemes and pension plans can also help in building your savings and generating income.
Conclusion
Learning how to increase income sources becomes easier when you accept that every financial journey has its share of struggles. What matters is taking steady steps that move you closer to your goals. Trying new income ideas, improving your skills, or adding small investments may help you gradually break free from money stress. Over time, these efforts build multiple income streams that may help to achieve various financial goals and handle any emergencies. With patience and smart choices, you can create a more flexible and secure financial life.
FAQs
How can I generate an additional source of income?
There are multiple options to increase income sources. You may start investing in FDs and bonds, real estate, or pension plans. You may leverage the internet to start a side hustle, such as selling digital products, creating courses, or teaching remotely.
What are the highest-earning sources of income?
There’s no single “best” option for everyone, but some sources can earn more than others. If you have sufficient funds, rental properties or dividend-paying stocks can grow your income steadily. If not, freelancing or consulting can also pay a handsome amount as you set your own rates and earn more for your skills.
What are the different sources of passive income?
Some sources of passive income are renting properties, high-interest savings plans, fixed deposits, creating digital courses, affiliate marketing, or starting an online business.
How can I increase my income using my skills or hobbies?
You can use your skills to teach online on your favourite subject topics, do freelance writing, or create social media posts on your interests, such as career advice, cooking videos, finance tips, or fashion. If you’re good at photography, you can monetise this hobby by posting regular pictures in a particular niche on stock photo platforms.
Disclaimer
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale. own or fund real estate that generates income. Usually, REITs hold commercial properties that have the potential to generate rental income. By investing in REITs, you can earn dividends from the rental income earned by the fund without having to buy the property. 2. Fixed Deposits and Bonds
Fixed Deposits remain a popular choice for low-risk investors because they’re stable, and offer assured returns. Invest in Unit Linked Insurance Plans (ULIPs)
ULIPs are life insurance plans which offer life insurance coverage and market-linked returns in a single plan. A portion of your premium provides insurance coverage, while the rest is invested in market-linked funds such as equity or debt. The maturity benefit received from a ULIP depends on how the market performs over time. You may invest in ULIPs, as they not only ensure financial protection but also help in growing your savings with market-linked returns. 4. Start a Side Hustle
Starting a side hustle may be one of the easiest ways to add a new income stream without major upfront costs. With just your skills, interest, some time, and an internet connection, you can tap into flexible earning options like freelance writing, bookkeeping, social media management, virtual assistance, or translation work. By offering your expertise on a project basis, you can choose the work you enjoy and often earn more than a traditional job would pay. It’s a flexible path that lets you turn your skills into a steady, independent income. 7. Offer Online Courses or Coaching
Thanks to digitisation, teaching online is another way to increase income sources. There’s always someone looking to learn, be it maths, English, cooking, design, or even soft skills. You don’t need much to begin, just a phone or laptop and a couple of hours a day. Over time, you can even expand by creating digital products such as e-books, courses, or templates that generate passive income. With consistency, this simple side gig can grow into a full-fledged online business.
Benefits of Increasing Your Income
Here are some benefits of having more than one source of income:
1. Reducing Stress
When you have more than one earning option, you’ll be in a better position to handle daily living costs, health issues, and fulfil financial goals. The better your finances are, the easier your fight will be in any sudden emergency, such as losing your primary source of income or incurring substantial hospital bills. 2. Making Your Income More Stable
Having more than one income stream makes your earnings steadier. When your money comes from different places, such as a small business, freelance work, or investment plans, you’re not dependent on just one source. This variety also helps you manage your finances more efficiently, pick up new skills, and build stronger professional connections. Overall, it adds stability while giving you more control over how you work and grow.
3. Assisting You in Reaching Your Goals
Multiple income streams make it easier to move closer to your financial goals, from short- term ones, such as a vacation, to long-term milestones like building a retirement corpus or buying a home. Extra earnings allow you to put more money into saving plans and explore better investment options, helping your fund grow faster.
How to Generate Income with Low Investment?
You can also create wealth with low investments. Here are some ways to achieve that:
1. Capital Markets
Capital markets provide opportunities to earn money with small investments. Here, you can buy shares of companies or invest in debt instruments like bonds that pay regular interest. While market movements do carry some risk, thoughtful planning and informed choices can help you grow your money over time. 2. Mutual Funds
Mutual funds pool money from many investors, and a professional fund manager invests it across stocks, bonds, or other assets. You contribute your amount, and the fund invests it in a diversified portfolio of securities. The best part is that you can start affordably through an SIP, adjust your investments as goals change, and even save taxes with ELSS under Section 80C. It’s an easy and flexible way to increase your savings.
3. Corporate Fixed Deposits
Corporate FDs are fixed-term deposits offered by NBFCs and certain financial institutions, where you park your money for a set period at a pre-decided interest rate. They offer attractive returns, with tenures ranging from a few months to several years. It’s wise to check the company has strong fundamentals and a reliable credit rating before you invest, as safety depends on the issuer’s stability. 4. National Pension Scheme
The National Pension System (NPS) is a retirement-focused investment option that helps you build a long-term corpus. Your contributions are spread across various asset classes such as equities, government securities, and corporate bonds, allowing you to choose the allocation based on your comfort with risk. NPS also offers tax benefits and comes with market-linked growth, making it a practical and low-cost way to save for the future. 5. Government Schemes
Government schemes include various investment avenues backed by the government. These investment schemes can offer guaranteed returns and also tax-benefits in certain cases. Some of the popular types of government schemes include Public Provident Fund, National Savings Certificates, Kisan Vikas Patra, etc. 6. Life insurance plans
Savings-oriented life insurance plans, like endowment, money back or ULIPs, can also help in generating income. money-back plans pay regular money back payouts that supplement your income. On the other hand, ULIPs offer the feature of partial withdrawals after the lock-in period of 5 years that allow you to access the fund value when needed. Then, there are endowment plans that might offer the monthly income option on policy benefits. 7. Pension Plans
A pension plan is a retirement-oriented long-term savings scheme, where you set aside money regularly to build up a retirement fund. Once you retire, you can use the accumulated retirement fund to buy an immediate annuity plan from a life insurance company and generate annuity income. You can choose from various options such as annuity plans offered by life insurance companies, Atal Pension Yojana, Senior Citizen Saving Scheme, etc. Early contributions benefit from compounding, helping you accumulate a corpus with time. Many plans also qualify for tax deductions under Sections 80C, 80CCC, and 80CCD, which makes retirement planning tax-efficient, too.
Key Takeaways
1. You need to increase income sources to beat inflation, handle hospitalisation and education
costs, save money for emergencies, and fulfil all financial goals without stress. 2. Investing in real estate, FDs, bonds, or ULIPs is one of the answers to ‘how to increase an
income source’. 3. You may use the internet to start a side hustle, teach online, start an online business, or
freelance your skills to earn passive income. 4. When you have more than one income source, you’re able to save more, achieve your
financial goals easily, and lead a stress-free life. 5. Some low investment plans to create a corpus are investing in the capital market, mutual
funds, or corporate fixed deposits. 6. Government schemes and pension plans can also help in building your savings and
generating income.
Conclusion
Learning how to increase income sources becomes easier when you accept that every financial journey has its share of struggles. What matters is taking steady steps that move you closer to your goals. Trying new income ideas, improving your skills, or adding small investments may help you gradually break free from money stress. Over time, these efforts build multiple income streams that may help to achieve various financial goals and handle any emergencies. With patience and smart choices, you can create a more flexible and secure financial life.

1. How can I generate an additional source of income?
There are multiple options to increase income sources. You may start investing in FDs and bonds, real estate, or pension plans. You may leverage the internet to start a side hustle, such as selling digital products, creating courses, or teaching remotely.
2. What are the highest-earning sources of income?
There’s no single “best” option for everyone, but some sources can earn more than others. If you have sufficient funds, rental properties or dividend-paying stocks can grow your income steadily. If not, freelancing or consulting can also pay a handsome amount as you set your own rates and earn more for your skills. 3. What are the different sources of passive income?
Some sources of passive income are renting properties, high-interest savings plans, fixed deposits, creating digital courses, affiliate marketing, or starting an online business. 4. How can I increase my income using my skills or hobbies?
You can use your skills to teach online on your favourite subject topics, do freelance writing, or create social media posts on your interests, such as career advice, cooking videos, finance tips, or fashion. If you’re good at photography, you can monetise this hobby by posting regular pictures in a particular niche on stock photo platforms.

changes.word_original: To bridge the gap between earnings and expenses, diversifying income sources is important.
changes.pdf_final: To bridge the gap between earnings and expenses, diversifying income sources is important. 2.

Usually, REITs hold commercial properties that have the potential to generate rental income.
By investing in REITs, you can earn dividends from the rental income earned by the fund without having to buy the property. 2.

changes.pdf_final: You contribute your amount, and the fund invests it in a diversified portfolio of securities.

changes.word_original: Company Fixed Deposits
Company FDs are fixed-term deposits offered by NBFCs and certain financial institutions, where you park your money for a set period at a pre-decided interest rate.
changes.pdf_final: Corporate Fixed Deposits
Corporate FDs are fixed-term deposits offered by NBFCs and certain financial institutions, where you park your money for a set period at a pre-decided interest rate.

changes.word_original: It’s wise to check the company has strong fundamentals and a reliable credit rating before you invest, as safety depends on the issuer’s stability.
changes.pdf_final: It’s wise to check the company has strong fundamentals and a reliable credit rating before you invest, as safety depends on the issuer’s stability. 4.

changes.word_original: Your contributions are spread across equities, government securities, and corporate bonds, allowing you to choose the allocation based on your comfort with risk.
changes.pdf_final: Your contributions are spread across various asset classes such as equities, government securities, and corporate bonds, allowing you to choose the allocation based on your comfort with risk.

changes.word_original: NPS also offers tax benefits and comes with market-linked growth, making it a practical and low-cost way to save for the future.
changes.pdf_final: NPS also offers tax benefits and comes with market-linked growth, making it a practical and low-cost way to save for the future. 5.

changes.word_original: Some of the popular types of government schemes include Public Provident Fund, National Savings Certificates, Kisan Vikas Patra, etc.
changes.pdf_final: Some of the popular types of government schemes include Public Provident Fund, National Savings Certificates, Kisan Vikas Patra, etc. 6.
Life insurance plans
Savings-oriented life insurance plans, like endowment, money back or ULIPs, can also help in generating income. money-back plans pay regular money back payouts that supplement your income.
On the other hand, ULIPs offer the feature of partial withdrawals after the lock-in period of 5 years that allow you to access the fund value when needed.
Then, there are endowment plans that might offer the monthly income option on policy benefits. 7.

changes.word_original: Once you retire, you can use the accumulated retirement fund to generate annuity income.
changes.pdf_final: Once you retire, you can use the accumulated retirement fund to buy an immediate annuity plan from a life insurance company and generate annuity income.

changes.word_original: You can choose from various options such as life insurance annuity plans, NPS, Atal Pension Yojana, Senior Citizen Saving Scheme, etc.
changes.pdf_final: You can choose from various options such as annuity plans offered by life insurance companies, Atal Pension Yojana, Senior Citizen Saving Scheme, etc.

changes.word_original: Early contributions benefit from compounding, helping you accumulate a good corpus with time.
changes.pdf_final: Early contributions benefit from compounding, helping you accumulate a corpus with time.
